judgmentEn: The Family. The perseverance of the woman furthers.
imageZh: 风自火出，家人；君子以言有物而行有恒。
imageEn: Wind comes forth from fire: the image of the Family. Thus the superior man has substance in his words and duration in his way of life.
interpretationZh: 家人卦象征家庭，风从火中生出，火旺则风生。治家之道在于言行一致，持之以恒。
interpretationEn: Jia Ren symbolizes the family. Wind emerges from fire — when the hearth burns bright, its influence spreads. Governing the family requires consistency in word and deed.

lines.textZh: 初九：闲有家，悔亡。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Firm seclusion within the family. Remorse disappears.
lines.interpretationZh: 治家之初即立规矩，防患于未然，悔恨消失。
lines.interpretationEn: Establish firm rules for the family from the start. Prevention removes remorse.
lines.detailZh: “闲有家，悔亡。”初爻为治家之始，“闲”有防闲、约束、立界限之意。家庭问题往往不是大祸突然降临，而是失于早期规范，纵容小失，终成积弊。初九阳刚居下，宜在开端就把规矩立住，把门内秩序安顿好。这里的关键不是苛刻，而是防微杜渐。若一开始就能厘清责任、收束散漫、建立基本礼法，则后续虽有摩擦，也不至于积成悔恨，所以曰“悔亡”。
lines.detailEn: “Keeping the household within proper bounds brings remorse to an end.” The first line concerns the beginning of family order. “Keeping within bounds” suggests setting limits early, preventing disorder before it grows. Most household troubles do not begin as disasters; they begin with small indulgences, vague expectations, and neglected discipline. This line advises establishing sound habits and clear boundaries from the start. The point is not harshness, but early correction. When the foundation is set properly, later regret can be avoided.

lines.textZh: 六二：无攸遂，在中馈，贞吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: She should not follow her whims. She must attend within to the food. Perseverance brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 不可随心所欲，专注于家中饮食之事，坚守正道则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Do not follow whims. Attend to nourishing the household. Perseverance brings fortune.
lines.detailZh: “无攸遂，在中馈，贞吉。”六二柔顺得中，处内卦中位，最能体现“利女贞”之义。“中馈”本指主持饮食内务，引申为安守内职、成就家庭运转的核心工作。此爻说不必外求功名，不必强作主张，只要守中而尽责，便可得吉。它赞美的是那种不张扬却不可或缺的稳定力量。现代来看，就是把看似琐碎的基础事务做好，让系统持续运转的人。
lines.detailEn: “She should not follow her whims. She attends to the food within. Constancy brings good fortune.” This yielding line in the central place of the lower trigram represents inner steadiness and proper domestic responsibility. “Attending to the food” literally refers to managing nourishment in the household, but more broadly it means sustaining the inner operations that keep life together. The teaching is not passivity, but faithful attention to essential duties. One need not chase outward recognition; by remaining centered and reliable in what truly matters, good fortune follows.

lines.textZh: 九三：家人嗃嗃，悔厉吉；妇子嘻嘻，终吝。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: When tempers flare up in the family, too great severity brings remorse. Good fortune nonetheless. When woman and child dally and laugh, it leads in the end to humiliation.
lines.interpretationZh: 治家严厉虽有悔恨但终究吉利；若妇孺嬉笑无度，终有困窘。
lines.interpretationEn: Strictness in the family may cause regret but leads to fortune. Excessive laxity ends in humiliation.
lines.detailZh: “家人嗃嗃，悔厉吉；妇子嘻嘻，终吝。”九三刚而过中，处家中要冲，最容易走向两种极端：一是过严，二是过纵。爻辞指出，家中若能严肃警惕，即便一时令人紧张，虽有悔厉，结果仍吉；但若上下嬉笑无度、失去分寸，看似和乐，最终反致羞吝。此爻强调治家不可只有温情而无规矩。真正的和谐并非毫无约束，而是在敬与爱之间取得平衡。
lines.detailEn: “When the family is severe and watchful, there may be regret and difficulty, yet in the end good fortune. If wife and children laugh and trifle, the end is humiliation.” This line warns against indulgent looseness. Though excessive severity carries risk, some seriousness is necessary to preserve order. Superficial cheerfulness without boundaries may look pleasant for a time, but it weakens respect and leads to disorder. The line teaches that authentic harmony includes discipline; affection without structure eventually becomes damaging.

lines.textZh: 六四：富家，大吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: She is the treasure of the house. Great good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 使家庭富裕兴旺，大吉大利。
lines.interpretationEn: She enriches the family. Great good fortune.
lines.detailZh: “富家，大吉。”六四居近君位，以柔得正，表示家庭已进入丰厚稳固之境。这里的“富”不只指财富丰盈，更包括家风充实、关系和顺、人才兴旺、资源有序。能富其家者，必先善于经营内在秩序，使一家人同心而不相耗。此爻所示之吉，是积累之后的自然结果：当家庭能彼此成就、减少内耗，物质与精神层面都会逐渐充实。
lines.detailEn: “Enriching the family brings great good fortune.” This line refers to the maturing stage of household life, when proper order begins to yield abundance. “Enrichment” means more than money; it includes cultivated values, mutual support, capable members, and a flourishing domestic atmosphere. Prosperity here is the fruit of reduced inner waste and increased harmony. When the household is well managed, both material and moral wealth can grow together.

lines.textZh: 九五：王假有家，勿恤，吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: As a king he approaches his family. Fear not. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 君王以仁爱治家，不必忧虑，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: The king approaches his family with love. No need to worry. Good fortune.
lines.detailZh: “王假有家，勿恤吉。”九五居尊位而得中，象征以德临家，如王者亲至宗庙家国之中。“假”有来到、感格之义，即主事者真正进入家庭核心，以诚意凝聚众人。此时不必忧虑，因为上位者若能以尊而不隔、以正而有恩，则全家自然归附。此爻也可引申为领导者治理组织时，要真正关心内部人心，而非只在外部发号施令。得其心者，故“勿恤吉”。
lines.detailEn: “The king approaches his family. Do not worry. Good fortune.” In the fifth place, authority is properly centered and morally effective. The ruler does not stand apart; he enters the heart of the family and unifies it through presence and sincerity. This line suggests leadership that is dignified yet caring, authoritative yet not alienated. When the one in charge truly gathers people through trust and moral gravity, anxiety subsides and good fortune follows.

lines.textZh: 上九：有孚威如，终吉。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: His work commands respect. In the end good fortune comes.
lines.interpretationZh: 有诚信且有威严，最终吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Sincerity combined with dignity. In the end, good fortune comes.
lines.detailZh: “有孚威如，终吉。”上九为家道之终，强调最后维系一家长久的，不只是亲情，而是“孚”与“威”并存。孚是诚信，使人愿意信服；威是庄重，使人不敢轻慢。若只有威而无孚，便成压制；只有孚而无威，则失于松弛。上九说明成熟的家长或领导，应以可信的品格建立权威，以稳定的原则维持秩序。如此到最后仍能保全其吉，故曰“终吉”。
lines.detailEn: “There is trust and a commanding presence. In the end, good fortune.” The top line gives the completed form of family authority: trust joined with dignity. Trust wins the heart; dignity preserves order. If there is only force, people submit outwardly but resist inwardly. If there is only warmth, they may relax into disorder. Lasting household leadership requires both credibility and gravity. When authority rests on genuine character, the good outcome endures to the end.

overviewZh: 家人卦，上巽下离，风自火出，象征家庭内部由内而外的教化与秩序。此卦不单指血缘之家，更指一切以伦理、责任、分工维系的小共同体。离为明，强调内在的礼法与情理；巽为入，表示风化流行、影响渐达。家人之道，在于各守其位、各尽其责，尤其重视内政有序、言行一致。卦辞称“利女贞”，并非狭义褒扬女性，而是指出治家之本在于柔顺中正、守常持正，家庭稳定常由内而定。若一家之中纲纪分明、情义不失，则能由齐家而推及治事、治国。反之，若只讲亲情不讲原则，或只讲威严不讲恩信，皆难久安。家人卦提醒人们：真正稳固的关系，不靠一时热情，而靠长期可依赖的分寸、责任与恒常之德。
overviewEn: Hexagram 37, The Family, consists of Wind above Fire. It describes how order, warmth, and moral influence arise from within a household and extend outward like wind emerging from flame. This hexagram is not limited to a literal family; it also applies to any close-knit unit held together by shared roles, trust, and responsibility. Fire suggests clarity, inner culture, and visible standards; Wind suggests penetration, gentle influence, and the spread of example. The core teaching is that harmony does not come from emotion alone, but from each person fulfilling a proper role with steadiness and sincerity. The phrase “beneficial for the woman’s constancy” points to the classical insight that the inner foundation of the home must be stable, disciplined, and enduring. In a broader sense, The Family teaches that good governance begins in the nearest relationships. If speech is grounded and conduct consistent, the household becomes a place of nourishment and moral formation. If affection lacks principle, or authority lacks trust, disorder follows. Thus the hexagram emphasizes sustained character, relational duty, and the quiet power of example.
judgmentDetailZh: 卦辞曰：“家人：利女贞。”《彖传》进一步解释：“家人，女正位乎内，男正位乎外。男女正，天地之大义也。家人有严君焉，父母之谓也。父父、子子、兄兄、弟弟、夫夫、妇妇，而家道正；正家而天下定矣。”其核心不在性别尊卑，而在“正位”二字。家之所以为家，不是因为同住一处，而是因为每个人都明白自己的本分与责任。所谓“利女贞”，强调内在秩序、持家之常、柔中有守，这是家庭稳定的根基。父母有教，子弟有承，夫妇有别而能相成，兄弟有序而不相夺，则家庭形成稳定的伦理结构。家人卦也说明，社会秩序并非空中楼阁，而是从日常关系中逐层建立。若一家人言语轻慢、责任失衡，即使外在富足，也难言吉。若能先正其家，再推其德，则小至家庭团队，大至社会组织，皆可得其治。
judgmentDetailEn: The Judgment says, “The Family. Constancy of the woman is beneficial.” The Tuan Commentary expands: “In The Family, the woman has her correct place within, and the man has his correct place without. When man and woman are correctly placed, this is the great principle of heaven and earth. In the family there is a stern ruler: this means the father and mother. When the father is truly father, the son truly son, the elder brother truly elder brother, the younger brother truly younger brother, the husband truly husband, and the wife truly wife, then the way of the family is correct; when the family is set in order, all under heaven is settled.” The essential point is not hierarchy for its own sake, but correctness of role. A family is not defined merely by living together, but by mutual responsibility and moral coherence. “Beneficial for the woman’s constancy” highlights the importance of the inner foundation: continuity, care, restraint, and faithful order. The hexagram teaches that governance begins in intimate relations. If people neglect role, duty, and reverence, even material comfort cannot secure peace. But when each relation becomes true to itself, the household becomes the seedbed of wider social order.
imageDetailZh: 《象传》曰：“风自火出，家人；君子以言有物而行有恒。”火在内而风在外，像炉中之火发出暖气，进而形成可感的流动，正如家庭内部的德行与规矩，会自然外化为一家人的气象。火主明，表示内在有礼有识；风主入，表示影响潜移默化，不靠强压，而靠日积月累。君子观此卦象，明白治家治人不能只靠口号，故“言有物”即说话有内容、有根据、不空泛；“行有恒”即行为稳定、一贯、不反复。家庭中最怕朝令夕改、言行相违。若内里温明而外在和顺，则一家之气自正。此象也提示，真正的家风不是写在墙上的训词，而是成员每天如何说话、做事、待人，这种无形之风最能塑造人心。
imageDetailEn: The Image says, “Wind comes forth from Fire: The Family. Thus the noble one has substance in words and constancy in conduct.” Fire burns within; wind issues forth without. The image suggests inner warmth and clarity giving rise to an outward atmosphere that can be felt by everyone nearby. In family life, what is internal—values, discipline, affection, reverence—naturally expresses itself in tone, habits, and shared culture. Fire represents illumination and discernment; wind represents influence that enters gently and spreads gradually. Thus the superior person does not rely on slogans or sudden authority. “Words with substance” means speech that is grounded, meaningful, and trustworthy. “Constancy in conduct” means stable action over time, not moods or contradictions. A family is shaped less by formal declarations than by repeated daily example. When the inside is clear and warm, the outside becomes harmonious. This image therefore teaches that lasting household order arises from embodied virtue, not mere control.
modernApplicationZh: 事业上，家人卦提醒团队管理要先立内部规则，再谈外部扩张。一个组织若职责不清、奖惩失衡、沟通混乱，就像家无家法，难成大事；反之，若领导以身作则、制度稳定、成员各安其位，团队执行力会明显增强。感情上，此卦强调关系的经营重在长期责任，不只是激情与浪漫。伴侣之间需明确边界、分担事务、守住信任，才能形成真正可持续的亲密关系。财运上，家人卦偏向“守成聚财”，适合通过稳定经营、家庭协作、预算管理来累积资源，不利于因一时冲动而进行高风险投机。健康上，则提示身心状态与家庭氛围密切相关，长期争执、失序作息、情绪压抑，都会消耗元气；若能改善居家节律、饮食起居和沟通方式，健康也会随之转稳。整体而言，家人卦在现代社会对应的是“建立可持续关系系统”的能力：把最小单元经营好，外部成果才有根基。
modernApplicationEn: In career matters, The Family advises building internal order before pursuing external expansion. A team with vague responsibilities, inconsistent standards, and chaotic communication resembles a household without structure: it may be busy, but it will not be truly effective. Strong leadership here means setting a reliable example, defining roles clearly, and sustaining fair routines. In relationships, the hexagram points beyond romance toward durable partnership. Love matures through trust, division of responsibility, emotional steadiness, and mutual respect for boundaries. In finances, this is a conserving and consolidating hexagram. Wealth grows through disciplined management, household cooperation, practical budgeting, and steady accumulation rather than reckless speculation. In health, it suggests that well-being is deeply tied to the domestic environment. Chronic tension at home, irregular living habits, and unspoken resentment can erode vitality over time. Restoring order in meals, rest, emotional communication, and physical space often improves health as well. Overall, The Family in modern life teaches the art of building sustainable systems of closeness: if the nearest circle is well ordered, work, love, money, and wellness all gain a more stable foundation.
historicalStoryZh: 可借周代重视宗法与家教的传统来理解家人卦。周公制礼作乐，不只是为朝廷立法，更是把国家秩序建立在家族伦理之上。古人讲“修身齐家治国平天下”，并非空泛格言，而是认为人若不能处理父子、夫妇、兄弟之间的关系，也很难治理更大的共同体。尤其曾国藩家书最能体现家人之义：他反复告诫子弟要勤俭、谨言、守礼、各安本分，不求一时显赫，先求门风端正。其家族能数代不坠，正因重“言有物，行有恒”。这类历史经验说明，家人卦所讲并非狭隘家庭主义，而是一种由近及远的治理逻辑：先整顿最亲近的关系，再扩展到事业与社会，方能久远。
historicalStoryEn: A useful historical lens for The Family is the Zhou tradition of ritual order rooted in kinship ethics. The Duke of Zhou did not merely regulate the court; he framed political order on the basis of properly ordered family relations. This is the spirit behind the classical sequence: cultivate the self, regulate the family, govern the state, bring peace to the world. A later and more concrete example appears in the family letters of Zeng Guofan. He repeatedly instructed his children and younger kin to be frugal, disciplined in speech, reverent in conduct, and faithful to their duties. He did not seek brilliance without foundation; he cared first about rectifying the family ethos. The endurance of his household reputation across generations illustrates the teaching of this hexagram: stable order grows from habitual integrity. The Family is therefore not narrow domesticism, but a model of how durable institutions begin in the smallest circle of trust and responsibility.
relatedHexagramsNote: 家人卦与第36卦明夷、第38卦睽相邻，意义连贯。明夷讲光明受伤，提示人在昏乱环境中先守内明；家人则进一步要求把内在秩序落实于家庭伦理；睽则指出若角色失衡、情志相违，亲近之人也会离心。三卦连看，可见由内守、到内治、到防离散的过程。
references: 《彖传》：家人，女正位乎内，男正位乎外……正家而天下定矣。
《象传》：风自火出，家人；君子以言有物而行有恒。

judgmentEn: Opposition. In small matters, good fortune.
imageZh: 上火下泽，睽；君子以同而异。
imageEn: Above, fire; below, the lake: the image of Opposition. Thus amid all fellowship the superior man retains his individuality.
interpretationZh: 睽卦象征乖离对立，火向上燃，泽水向下流，方向相背。小事可成，大事宜慎。君子求同存异。
interpretationEn: Kui symbolizes opposition. Fire flames upward, the lake seeps downward — they move apart. Small matters succeed. The superior man maintains individuality within fellowship.

lines.textZh: 初九：悔亡，丧马勿逐，自复；见恶人，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Remorse disappears. If you lose your horse, do not run after it; it will come back of its own accord. When you see evil people, guard yourself against mistakes.
lines.interpretationZh: 悔恨消失。丢失马匹不必追赶，它会自己回来。见到恶人，谨慎应对则无咎。
lines.interpretationEn: Remorse vanishes. A lost horse returns on its own. When meeting evil people, guard against error.
lines.detailZh: ‘悔亡，丧马勿逐，自复；见恶人无咎。’初九处睽之始，虽有失散，但尚未酿成大害。所谓丧马勿逐，是说有些失去不必急追，顺其势反能自行归复。见恶人无咎，则指在局势不明时，暂与不喜之人接触，也不必先入为主，只要守住分寸即可免咎。此爻启示：关系初裂时，忌躁进，宜观察，留有回旋余地。
lines.detailEn: ‘Regret vanishes. If you lose the horse, do not chase it; it returns by itself. Seeing an unpleasant person brings no blame.’ At the beginning of estrangement, loss has appeared but has not yet hardened into disaster. The advice not to chase the horse means that not every separation should be pursued aggressively; some things return when pressure is removed. Meeting a disagreeable person without blame suggests that in unclear conditions, even contact with those one dislikes may be acceptable if one remains measured. The line teaches patience and restraint at the first sign of division.

lines.textZh: 九二：遇主于巷，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: One meets his lord in a narrow street. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 在小巷中偶遇主人，不期而遇，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Meeting one's lord in a narrow lane. An unexpected encounter. No blame.
lines.detailZh: ‘遇主于巷，无咎。’九二居中得正，在睽时仍能与主相遇，虽非堂皇正式之场合，只在巷中相逢，却已足见联系未断。此爻说明，在分歧阶段，不必拘泥形式，只要核心关系、关键沟通尚在，就有转圜余地。真正重要的是诚意相接，而不是场面是否完美。
lines.detailEn: ‘Meeting the lord in a lane—no blame.’ Nine in the second place is centered and appropriate, showing that even in estrangement, meaningful connection can still occur. The meeting happens not in a grand court but in an alley, implying that reconciliation may begin informally, quietly, and without ceremony. This line teaches that during divided times, preserving essential communication matters more than maintaining perfect appearances. Sincere contact outweighs formal setting.

lines.textZh: 六三：见舆曳，其牛掣，其人天且劓，无初有终。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: One sees the wagon dragged back, the oxen halted, a man's hair and nose cut off. Not a good beginning, but a good end.
lines.interpretationZh: 看到车被拖住，牛被阻止，人受刑罚。开头不好但结局尚可。
lines.interpretationEn: The wagon is dragged back, the oxen halted, the man punished. A bad start but a good end.
lines.detailZh: ‘见舆曳，其牛掣，其人天且劓，无初有终。’六三阴柔失位，又处上下阻隔之间，故所见尽是牵制、羞辱与受困之象。车被拖曳，牛被牵掣，人受刑伤，皆喻事情推进艰难，关系中夹杂误解与压迫。然‘无初有终’表明开始虽坏，若能熬过最艰困阶段，终仍可有结果。此爻重在警示：当处境最拧巴时，不可因羞愤而自毁全局。
lines.detailEn: ‘One sees the cart dragged back, the oxen halted, and the person branded and mutilated. No good beginning, but there is an end.’ Six in the third place occupies an awkward and unstable position, caught amid obstruction above and below. The imagery of the dragged cart, restrained oxen, and punished person conveys frustration, humiliation, and painful delay. Yet the phrase ‘no beginning, but an end’ shows that a troubled start does not forbid eventual completion. This line warns against despair or self-destruction when circumstances feel most tangled and oppressive.

lines.textZh: 九四：睽孤，遇元夫，交孚，厉无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: Isolated through opposition, one meets a like-minded man with whom one can associate in good faith. Despite the danger, no blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 因乖离而孤独，遇到志同道合之人，以诚信相交，虽有危险但无咎。
lines.interpretationEn: Isolated by opposition, one meets a kindred spirit. Sincere fellowship amid danger brings no blame.
lines.detailZh: ‘睽孤，遇元夫，交孚，厉无咎。’九四在外卦之初，有孤立之感，但能遇到志同而可靠之人，彼此以诚信相交，虽局势仍危，却可免于灾咎。此爻说明，在分歧环境中，最重要的不是获得多数附和，而是找到真正可信的伙伴。只要交信不欺，即使处境艰难，也能稳住局面。
lines.detailEn: ‘Alone in estrangement, one meets a great man. Through mutual trust there is danger, but no blame.’ Nine in the fourth place feels isolated, standing in the outer trigram with limited support. Yet it encounters a worthy counterpart, and through trustful exchange avoids misfortune despite real danger. The line emphasizes that in times of opposition, survival does not depend on broad approval but on finding one trustworthy ally. Genuine confidence can stabilize even a precarious situation.

lines.textZh: 六五：悔亡，厥宗噬肤，往何咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Remorse disappears. The companion bites his way through the wrappings. If one goes to him, how could it be a mistake?
lines.interpretationZh: 悔恨消失，同宗之人穿透阻隔来亲近，前往则无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Remorse vanishes. The companion breaks through barriers. Going to meet him — how could that be wrong?
lines.detailZh: ‘悔亡，厥宗噬肤，往何咎。’六五居尊位而以柔中应下，故能化解前悔。‘厥宗噬肤’比喻亲近之人帮助打通阻隔，如咬破柔肤般虽有触动，却能迅速见效。往何咎，说明此时宜主动前往、恢复联结，不必迟疑。此爻所示，是高位者若肯放下隔膜、借助亲近可靠的力量，便能重建关系。
lines.detailEn: ‘Regret vanishes. One’s kindred bites through the skin. Going forward, what blame could there be?’ Six in the fifth place is yielding yet central, able to soften estrangement from a position of influence. The strange image of relatives biting through skin suggests close assistance that penetrates obstruction quickly and directly. Therefore advancing brings no blame. This line teaches that a leader or central figure who humbly relies on trusted, intimate support can reopen blocked relationships and dissolve prior regret.

lines.textZh: 上九：睽孤，见豕负涂，载鬼一车，先张之弧，后说之弧，匪寇婚媾，往遇雨则吉。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: Isolated through opposition, one sees one's companion as a pig covered with dirt, as a wagon full of devils. First one draws a bow against him, then one lays the bow aside. He is not a robber; he will woo at the right time. As one goes, rain falls; then good fortune comes.
lines.interpretationZh: 因乖离而孤立，误解同伴，先敌视后释然。对方并非敌人而是求婚者，前行遇雨则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Isolated and suspicious, one misjudges a companion. Hostility gives way to understanding. Going forward, rain falls and fortune comes.
lines.detailZh: ‘睽孤，见豕负涂，载鬼一车，先张之弧，后说之弧，匪寇婚媾，往遇雨则吉。’上九居睽之极，猜疑最深，故眼中所见皆似妖妄可怖：猪背泥污，满车如鬼，先欲张弓防敌。然最终发现并非寇仇，而是婚媾之来，误会解除；又逢雨而吉，象征阴阳和洽、燥气得润。此爻深刻说明，睽之极处最易把他人看成敌人，但若能放下过度戒备，真相显明后，反而迎来转机与和合。
lines.detailEn: ‘Alone in estrangement, one sees a pig covered with mud and a cart full of ghosts. First one draws the bow, then puts it aside. It is not robbers but a marriage procession. Going on, one meets rain, and it is auspicious.’ At the top of Kui, estrangement reaches its extreme and suspicion distorts perception. Everything appears threatening or grotesque, so the impulse is to arm oneself. Yet the supposed enemy proves to be no bandit at all, but a bearer of union. The rain signifies release, reconciliation, and the joining of separated forces. This line reveals how, at the height of alienation, misjudgment is most likely. When defensive fear is relaxed and reality is seen clearly, hostility can turn into blessing.

overviewZh: 睽卦为《易经》第三十八卦，上离下兑，火在上而泽在下，二者性质相违，象征背离、分歧与相互观望。然睽并非单纯的冲突之卦，它所揭示的是在差异中寻找秩序、在不合中保持原则。卦辞曰‘小事吉’，说明当大局尚未完全统一时，不宜强行整合，更适合从细微处修补关系、化解隔阂。睽的核心不是消灭差异，而是承认差异、驾驭差异。人与人之间、群体之间、理念之间往往难以完全一致，但只要能够守住诚信、辨明是非，就仍有合作与转机。此卦提醒人们，在意见相左、情感疏离或道路分岔之时，不可因一时对立而走向决裂；真正的智慧，在于于异中求同，于纷乱中守中道。
overviewEn: Hexagram 38, Kui, is composed of Li above and Dui below: fire rising upward and marsh settling downward. Their movements differ, so the hexagram signifies divergence, estrangement, and opposition. Yet Kui is not merely about conflict. It teaches how to remain clear-minded and principled when people, interests, or perspectives no longer align. The Judgment says, ‘Small matters are auspicious,’ suggesting that when broad unity cannot yet be restored, one should not force a grand resolution. Instead, progress comes through modest repairs, limited agreements, and patient clarification. The deeper wisdom of Kui lies in recognizing that difference does not always mean destruction. In human affairs, complete sameness is rare; what matters is whether integrity, trust, and discernment can still be preserved. This hexagram advises restraint during periods of disagreement, emotional distance, or mutual suspicion. Rather than escalating division, one should seek points of contact within difference, maintain moral center amid confusion, and allow reconciliation to emerge through sincerity and measured action.
judgmentDetailZh: 卦辞‘睽：小事吉’言简义深，指出睽时之道不在大举妄动，而在谨慎应对。彖传说：‘睽，火动而上，泽动而下，二女同居，其志不同行；说而丽乎明，柔进而上行，得中而应乎刚，是以小事吉。天地睽而其事同也，男女睽而其志通也，万物睽而其事类也；睽之时用大矣哉。’这段话说明，睽的本质是表面相违、内里仍可相通。天地运行方向不同，却共同成就化育；男女气质不同，却能相感相成；万物形态各异，却各归其类。故睽不是绝对的坏局，而是一种需要辨析结构、重建连接的时势。所谓‘小事吉’，是因为在分歧明显之时，若仍妄图一次性解决根本问题，往往适得其反；反而从小处入手，借中正、柔顺与诚信，先恢复基本信任，才能为后续转合创造条件。
judgmentDetailEn: The Judgment, ‘Kui: small matters are auspicious,’ is brief but profound. It indicates that in times of estrangement, success does not come from sweeping action but from careful handling. The Tuan Commentary states: ‘Kui means fire moves upward and marsh moves downward. Two daughters dwell together, yet their wills do not go the same way. Yet there is joy joined to brightness; the yielding advances and moves upward, obtains the center, and responds to the firm. Therefore small matters are auspicious. Heaven and earth are opposed, yet their work is united. Man and woman are opposed, yet their intentions communicate. The myriad things are opposed, yet their functions remain of one kind. Great indeed is the usefulness of the time of Kui.’ This explains that opposition may appear outwardly, while inward correspondence still remains possible. Heaven and earth differ in position, yet together generate life. Human beings differ in temperament, yet can still form meaningful union. Thus Kui is not pure misfortune; it is a condition requiring discernment, proportion, and re-linking. ‘Small matters are auspicious’ because when divisions are visible, forcing total resolution often worsens matters. Modest, sincere, centered actions can restore trust first, making greater harmony possible later.
imageDetailZh: 象曰：‘上火下泽，睽；君子以同而异。’火炎上，泽润下，二者背向而行，形成睽离之象。君子观此，不强求表面一致，而明白事物本有差别，因此在大的原则上求同，在具体表现上容异。所谓‘同而异’，并非混同是非，而是在共同目标、共同道义之下，允许意见、方法、个性各有不同。睽卦之象也提醒人：分歧并不可怕，可怕的是失去包容与辨别。当环境充满误解与隔膜时，更要保持光明之德与和悦之心，以清楚的判断面对差异，以柔和的态度维系关系。如此，异不至于乱，同不至于僵，便能在对立结构中找到可持续的相处之道。
imageDetailEn: The Image says, ‘Fire above, marsh below: this is Kui. Thus the noble one preserves sameness in principle while allowing difference in expression.’ Fire flames upward while the marsh settles downward; their tendencies diverge, creating the image of separation. From this, the noble person learns not to demand superficial uniformity. Real unity does not erase distinctions. Instead, one seeks common moral ground while accepting diversity in temperament, method, and viewpoint. This is the meaning of being ‘united yet different.’ Kui teaches that disagreement itself is not the true danger; the greater danger is losing clarity, tolerance, or proportion. In periods marked by misunderstanding or estrangement, one must combine the brightness of discernment with the gentleness of measured conduct. Clear judgment prevents confusion, while openness prevents hardening into hostility. In this way, difference does not become chaos, and unity does not become rigidity. The image therefore presents a mature model of coexistence: shared direction without forced sameness.
modernApplicationZh: 在现代处境中，睽卦最适合解释人与系统、人与人之间‘目标未必一致，但关系仍可经营’的状态。事业上，团队理念不合、上下级认知错位、合作方标准不同，皆属睽象。此时不宜硬碰硬争胜，应先处理具体问题、厘清职责、建立最小共识，小处修通，大局才有转机。感情上，睽并非必然分手，更像彼此有距离、误会积累、表达方式不同。若能坦诚沟通、减少猜测、尊重差异，关系仍可修复；若执意要求对方完全照自己行事，则反而渐行渐远。财运上，睽示意市场判断分化，投资宜谨慎，适合小额试探、控制风险，不宜孤注一掷。健康上，多与情绪郁结、作息紊乱、内热外燥有关，提醒调节心火、舒缓压力，并关注眼睛、口腔、消化系统等问题。总体而言，睽卦告诉现代人：当外部失衡时，与其急于统一，不如先学会在分歧中维持秩序，在差异中寻找合作的接口。
modernApplicationEn: In modern life, Hexagram 38 often describes situations in which goals, values, or rhythms do not fully align, yet the relationship or structure can still be managed. In career matters, Kui appears when teams disagree on direction, leaders and staff misunderstand each other, or partners operate by different standards. The wise response is not head-on confrontation but practical clarification: define roles, solve concrete issues, and build minimal consensus. Repair the small channels first, and larger cooperation may return. In relationships, Kui does not automatically mean breakup; more often it points to emotional distance, accumulated misunderstanding, or incompatible styles of expression. Honest conversation, reduced suspicion, and respect for difference can still restore warmth. Financially, the hexagram suggests divided market sentiment and warns against impulsive bets. Small-scale testing, risk control, and patient observation are preferable to all-or-nothing moves. In health, Kui may correspond to stress, inner irritation, poor rhythm, or imbalance between mental stimulation and physical ease; attention should be given to sleep, emotional regulation, and systems linked with heat and dryness, such as the eyes, mouth, and digestion. Overall, Kui teaches modern people that when harmony breaks down, one should first preserve order within difference rather than force premature unity.
historicalStoryZh: 可借春秋战国间苏秦合纵受挫而终能说动六国的故事来理解睽卦。彼时诸侯彼此猜疑，利益不同，强弱悬殊，表面上各怀异志，正是‘睽’的局面。苏秦若一开始便空谈宏大战略，未必有人肯信；他真正成功之处，在于逐国分析利害，顺着各国不同处境分别游说，从小处着手建立共识，最终形成暂时联盟。这个过程正合‘小事吉’之义：分歧不可能一夜消失，但可以通过因势利导、分别沟通、逐步修补来达成阶段性合作。睽卦所强调的，不是把所有人变成同一种人，而是即使立场不同，也能因共同现实而暂时会合。历史上许多转机，都不是从绝对一致开始，而是从承认差异、利用差异开始的。
historicalStoryEn: A useful historical parallel for Hexagram 38 is the story of Su Qin during the Warring States period, when he promoted the Vertical Alliance among rival states. The political climate was one of suspicion, conflicting interests, and unequal power—an unmistakable condition of Kui. If Su Qin had begun with abstract rhetoric about total unity, few would have listened. His success came from understanding each state’s distinct fears and advantages, then persuading them one by one according to their separate situations. This reflects the teaching that ‘small matters are auspicious’: deep divisions are not erased in a day, but practical alignment can emerge through tailored communication and incremental trust-building. Kui does not demand that all parties become identical. Rather, it shows how different positions may still converge around shared necessity. Many historical turning points began not with perfect agreement, but with the wise recognition of difference and the skillful use of it.
relatedHexagramsNote: 睽与家人卦相综：家人重内部秩序与伦理整合，睽则写秩序松动后的分歧状态，二者前后相承。又与同人卦可参看：同人讲公开聚合，睽讲暂时离异中仍求可通之理，因此理解睽，须知真正的‘同’并非无差别，而是能容异守中。
references: 《彖传》：睽，火动而上，泽动而下……睽之时用大矣哉
《象传》：上火下泽，睽；君子以同而异

judgmentZh: 蹇：利西南，不利东北。利见大人，贞吉。
judgmentEn: Obstruction. The southwest furthers. The northeast does not further. It furthers one to see the great man. Perseverance brings good fortune.
imageZh: 山上有水，蹇；君子以反身修德。
imageEn: Water on the mountain: the image of Obstruction. Thus the superior man turns his attention to himself and molds his character.
interpretationZh: 蹇卦象征艰难险阻，山上有水，行路艰难。宜向平坦之处（西南）前进，反省自身，修养德行。
interpretationEn: Jian symbolizes obstruction. Water on the mountain makes the path treacherous. Move toward the easy ground. Turn inward and cultivate virtue.

lines.textZh: 初六：往蹇，来誉。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: Going leads to obstructions, coming meets with praise.
lines.interpretationZh: 前往则遇险阻，退回则获赞誉。宜暂时退守。
lines.interpretationEn: Going forward meets obstruction; turning back wins praise. Retreat for now.
lines.detailZh: “往蹇，来誉。”初六居蹇之始，才柔位下，若贸然前往，立刻遭遇险阻；若知难而返，则反得称誉。此爻强调处局势初困之时，最重要的是迅速识别方向错误，不把逞强当勇敢。能及时止损、保存元气，本身就是明智之举。
lines.detailEn: “Going brings obstruction; coming back brings praise.” At the beginning of difficulty, the line is weak and low in position. If it presses ahead, it meets obstruction at once; if it turns back in time, it earns respect. This line teaches the value of early recognition: correcting course before deeper loss is not cowardice but wisdom.

lines.textZh: 六二：王臣蹇蹇，匪躬之故。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: The king's servant is beset by obstruction upon obstruction, but it is not his own fault.
lines.interpretationZh: 王的臣子历经重重艰难，并非因为自身的缘故，而是为了尽忠职守。
lines.interpretationEn: The king's servant faces obstruction after obstruction, not through personal fault but from devotion to duty.
lines.detailZh: “王臣蹇蹇，匪躬之故。”六二得中而应上，虽处艰难，却是为公任事，不是为一己私利而劳苦。此爻指出，若所担之难是职责所在、使命所系，则虽重重阻碍，亦当守位尽责。蹇蹇者，言其难之深、忧之切，但因其动机正大，故有道义价值。
lines.detailEn: “The king’s servant is hindered upon hindered, not for his own sake.” This line portrays one who endures repeated difficulty in service of a higher duty. The hardship is not personal ambition but responsibility. It teaches that some obstructions must be borne because they belong to one’s rightful role and moral commitment.

lines.textZh: 九三：往蹇，来反。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: Going leads to obstructions; hence he comes back.
lines.interpretationZh: 前往则遇阻碍，因此返回。宜退守等待时机。
lines.interpretationEn: Going forward meets obstruction, so one turns back. Wait for the right moment.
lines.detailZh: “往蹇来反。”九三以阳居刚，处下卦之上，临近险地，最易生出强行突破之心。然而此时前进必陷，回返才是正道。此爻告诫有能力、有主见之人，越在关键节点越要克制自恃，不可因自信而误判时势。能反者，方能保全并待后图。
lines.detailEn: “Going leads to obstruction; returning leads back.” Strong in character, this line may be tempted to force a breakthrough. Yet the terrain ahead is too dangerous. The teaching here is especially for the capable: strength without timing becomes error. Withdrawal is not defeat but preservation for a better moment.

lines.textZh: 六四：往蹇，来连。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: Going leads to obstructions, coming leads to union.
lines.interpretationZh: 前往则遇阻碍，退回则能与人联合。宜团结合作。
lines.interpretationEn: Going forward meets obstruction; turning back leads to union. Seek allies.
lines.detailZh: “往蹇来连。”六四已近上体，处于承上启下之位。前进仍难，但回转则能与同道相连，获得协助。此爻说明，人在困局中不必孤军奋战；适时回身、重建连接，反而能形成支持网络。蹇时最怕孤立，最宜合力。
lines.detailEn: “Going leads to obstruction; coming back leads to connection.” Near the upper trigram, this line shows that direct advance still fails, but returning allows one to reconnect with allies. The message is that in difficulty, retreat can restore relationship, coordination, and support. Isolation worsens obstruction; connection begins release.

lines.textZh: 九五：大蹇，朋来。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: In the midst of the greatest obstructions, friends come.
lines.interpretationZh: 身处最大的困难之中，朋友前来相助。
lines.interpretationEn: In the greatest obstruction, friends arrive to help.
lines.detailZh: “大蹇朋来。”九五居尊位而中正，所遇之难最大，却也最能感召同志来助。大蹇意味着局势之重非一人可解，因此必须以正道与公心凝聚伙伴。此爻告诉领导者：真正的领导力，不在独扛一切，而在危难中仍能使贤者愿意前来共济。
lines.detailEn: “In great obstruction, friends come.” The ruler’s place meets the greatest difficulty, yet because it is central and correct, help arrives. This line speaks to leadership under pressure: when the cause is just and the center holds firm, capable allies gather. Great difficulty is overcome through shared effort, not solitary strain.

lines.textZh: 上六：往蹇，来硕，吉；利见大人。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: Going leads to obstructions, coming leads to great good fortune. It furthers one to see the great man.
lines.interpretationZh: 前往则遇阻碍，退回则有大收获，吉利。宜拜见大人物。
lines.interpretationEn: Going meets obstruction; returning brings great fortune. It furthers one to see the great man.
lines.detailZh: “往蹇来硕，吉；利见大人。”上六处蹇之终，若再向前，已无路可进；若能回归根本，则有丰硕之成，并得吉祥。此爻总结全卦之旨：困极之时，转身求正、求贤，反而迎来真正出路。所谓“硕”，不仅是成果之大，也指经历艰难后眼界与德量之充实。
lines.detailEn: “Going brings obstruction; coming back brings greatness. Good fortune. It is favorable to see the great person.” At the end of obstruction, there is no value in continuing the same direction. Turning back to what is fundamental opens the way to substantial gain. This final line completes the hexagram’s lesson: in extreme difficulty, wise reversal and worthy guidance create the true breakthrough.

overviewZh: 第39卦为蹇卦，上坎下艮，象为山上有水，路险而难行。坎为险陷，艮为止息，合而成义，即人在前行途中遇到阻滞、险难与进退失据之局。蹇并非纯凶之卦，它提示人于困顿之际，不可逞强冒进，而应知止、知返、知所依。卦辞言“利西南，不利东北”，意在说明顺势而行、趋平易之地则可通，逆险强进则愈加困顿；“利见大人”，则强调艰难时须借助贤能、秉正而行。此卦的深意不只在外部障碍，更在借阻力反观自身、修整德性与方法。所谓真正的通达，往往不是硬闯得来，而是在困局中学会调整方向、整合助力、沉潜蓄势之后，自然开路。故蹇卦是一卦教人面对现实、反身修德、在艰险中谋转机的智慧之卦。
overviewEn: Hexagram 39, Jian (Obstruction), is formed by Water above Mountain. Its image is water gathering on a mountain: danger ahead, stillness below, and movement made difficult. The hexagram speaks of impediments, delays, and circumstances in which pushing forward blindly only deepens the problem. Yet Jian is not simply unfortunate; it teaches the wisdom of intelligent restraint. When the road is blocked, one must know when to stop, when to turn back, and when to seek support. The Judgment says, “Favorable in the southwest, unfavorable in the northeast,” suggesting that progress comes through following the easier, more receptive path rather than forcing one’s way into greater hazard. It is also “fruitful to see the great person,” meaning that in times of difficulty, guidance, moral clarity, and alliance with capable people are essential. The deeper lesson of Jian is that outer obstacles reveal inner posture. Hardship becomes a mirror: it shows impatience, pride, rigidity, or lack of preparation. Properly understood, obstruction becomes a moment for self-cultivation, strategic retreat, and renewal. Thus Jian is a hexagram of difficulty transformed through humility, reflection, and right alignment.
judgmentDetailZh: 卦辞曰：“蹇：利西南，不利东北；利见大人，贞吉。”《彖传》解释说：“蹇，难也，险在前也。见险而能止，知矣哉！蹇利西南，往得中也；不利东北，其道穷也。利见大人，往有功也。当位贞吉，以正邦也。蹇之时用大矣哉！”所谓蹇，是前有险阻，行动失利之象。最重要的不在于一味突破，而在于“见险而能止”，这是高度的智慧。西南象征平顺、众助与中道，东北象征艮止与险难叠加，因此卦辞劝人避开不利方向，选择有转圜余地的道路。又言“利见大人”，说明处难时不可闭门独抗，而应借贤者之识、正者之力，以成其功。“贞吉”并非僵守，而是在艰难中守正不邪、守道不乱。此卦尤其强调时势判断：处蹇之时，退一步、缓一步、正一步，往往比急进更接近成功。蹇卦的价值，在于教人于穷困中仍不失正道，并懂得以变通成就长远之通。
judgmentDetailEn: The Judgment reads: “Obstruction. Favorable in the southwest, unfavorable in the northeast. Fruitful to see the great person. Constancy brings good fortune.” The Tuan Commentary explains: “Jian means difficulty; danger lies ahead. To see danger and know how to stop—this is wisdom indeed. Favorable in the southwest means going there gains the center; unfavorable in the northeast means the way is exhausted. Fruitful to see the great person means going forth then achieves merit. Constancy in the proper place brings good fortune, for thereby the state is set in order. Great indeed is the use of the time of Obstruction.” This commentary shows that Jian is not merely about bad luck, but about correct response to hardship. The noble action is not reckless persistence but recognizing peril and pausing before ruin. The southwest symbolizes cooperation, balance, and accessible terrain, while the northeast suggests deadlock and compounded resistance. “Seeing the great person” points to the need for wise counsel, moral authority, or alliance with capable leadership. “Constancy” here does not mean stubbornness; it means remaining aligned with what is right amid pressure and confusion. Jian therefore teaches strategic realism: when conditions are blocked, one succeeds not by force but by discernment, support, and principled adjustment. Its timing is profound because obstruction, rightly met, becomes the beginning of eventual achievement.
imageDetailZh: 《象传》曰：“山上有水，蹇；君子以反身修德。”山本高峻，水本流动，今水在山上，不得畅行，故成蹇象。此象呈现一种外有险势、内有止意的状态：前路崎岖，举步维艰，若仍躁进，只会陷入更深困境。君子观此，不急于怨天尤人，而是“反身修德”，把外在阻碍化作内在检点的契机。所谓反身，不仅是反思过失，更是审视自己的动机、方法与节奏是否失当；所谓修德，也不仅是道德训诫，而是提升承压能力、判断能力与处世分寸。蹇卦之象提醒人，当环境不顺时，最有力量的行动往往不是对抗世界，而是先整理自己。自我一旦端正，方向便 clearer，助力亦会逐渐汇聚。
imageDetailEn: The Image Commentary says: “Water on the mountain: Obstruction. Thus the noble person turns inward and cultivates virtue.” A mountain is naturally high and still; water naturally flows downward and freely. When water is on the mountain, movement is impeded and its normal course is disrupted. This creates the image of obstruction: difficult terrain, unstable footing, and forward motion made hazardous. The noble person, seeing this, does not waste energy in blame or agitation. Instead, external blockage becomes a call to inward correction. To “turn inward” means more than self-criticism; it is an examination of one’s motives, methods, timing, and emotional posture. To “cultivate virtue” means strengthening character, patience, clarity, and proper measure under stress. Jian’s image teaches that when circumstances resist us, the first and most effective correction may be within ourselves. Once inner order is restored, outer paths often become clearer. In this way, the image transforms hardship from mere frustration into a discipline of refinement and maturity.
modernApplicationZh: 在现代生活中，蹇卦常对应项目受阻、关系僵持、资金卡顿或身心疲惫的阶段。事业上，宜先评估风险与资源，不可在错误方向上持续加码；若团队、政策或市场环境明显不利，应调整路径，寻求资深人士、关键伙伴或跨部门协助，以迂回代替硬攻。感情上，蹇象多主沟通不畅、彼此防御或现实压力介入，此时最忌情绪化逼迫，应先退一步理解对方处境，修正表达方式，必要时借助可信的中间人或共同目标来重建连接。财运上，此卦不利冒险投资与冲动扩张，适合保守理财、清理债务、优化现金流，宁可慢而稳，不可逆势赌运。健康上，则提示慢性疲劳、筋骨关节、肾水代谢或压力累积等问题，关键在于停止透支，恢复作息，循序调养。蹇卦的现代启示是：遇阻时最需要的不是更猛，而是更准；不是逞强，而是修正、借力与等待时机。
modernApplicationEn: In modern life, Hexagram 39 often appears when plans stall, relationships become strained, finances tighten, or the body and mind feel overburdened. In career matters, it advises a sober review of risk, structure, and available support. Do not keep investing energy into a clearly blocked route. If the team, policy environment, or market conditions are unfavorable, change strategy, reduce exposure, and seek help from experienced mentors, senior decision-makers, or reliable partners. In relationships, Jian points to emotional distance, defensive communication, or practical pressures interfering with closeness. The worst approach is to force resolution through pressure or accusation. Better results come from patience, clearer communication, empathy, and sometimes mediation or a shared constructive goal. Financially, the hexagram warns against speculative expansion, impulsive deals, or trying to recover losses through greater risk. Conservative management, debt reduction, and liquidity protection are wiser. In health, it may point to exhaustion, stress accumulation, joint or mobility issues, or imbalance in recovery. Rest, rhythm, and gradual repair matter more than heroic effort. The modern lesson of Jian is simple but demanding: when blocked, do not merely push harder—adjust more wisely.
historicalStoryZh: 若以历史意象比附蹇卦，最贴近者之一是周文王困于羑里之时。身处拘囚、强敌在前、志业未伸，看似正处“险在前也”的极难局面。然而文王并未因困厄而躁进，更未轻举妄动，而是在艰难中整理天道、人事与王政之理，相传演绎《周易》，蓄德养望。其所行正合“见险而能止”与“反身修德”之义。待时势稍转，贤才归附，终使周室基础渐成。此类故事说明，真正的大成往往并不生于一路顺遂，而是在受困之际仍能守正、修德、待时、聚人。蹇之可贵，不在一时脱困，而在困而不乱、难而不失其道。
historicalStoryEn: A fitting historical image for Hexagram 39 is King Wen of Zhou during his imprisonment at Youli. With powerful danger before him and his larger mission seemingly obstructed, he stood in exactly the kind of difficult situation Jian describes. Yet he did not respond with reckless action or emotional collapse. Instead, within confinement he reflected deeply on the patterns of Heaven, human affairs, and political order, and tradition associates this period with his development of the Zhou Yi. This is the spirit of “seeing danger and knowing how to stop” and of “turning inward to cultivate virtue.” When the time later shifted, worthy people gathered around him and the foundations of Zhou strength were laid. Such a story shows that great achievement is not always born in smooth progress. Sometimes it is formed in hardship, through restraint, moral steadiness, preparation, and the ability to gather support without losing one’s way.
relatedHexagramsNote: 蹇卦常与第40卦“解”对观：蹇言险阻未解，解言舒缓解除，二者构成由困到通的次第。又可联系第29卦“坎”，同样论险，但坎重习险之中求行，蹇则重见险知止、反身修德。理解这几卦的连贯性，有助把握困境中的节奏与转机。

overviewZh: 解卦为《易经》第四十卦，上震下坎，雷动于水上，象征阴霾消散、险难得解。此卦的核心不在猛烈进攻，而在于历经危局后，顺势松绑、化险为夷。坎为险，震为动，表示困局并非静止不变，只要时机一到，行动得宜，便可突破僵局。解不是彻底放纵，也不是消极逃避，而是在紧张之后恢复秩序、在冲突之后重建信任。它提醒人们：危难解除之后，最重要的不是恋战，而是回归正道、安顿人心。若前路暂无新目标，不妨先休养整顿；若确有应行之事，则应趁时而动，不可迟缓。故解卦带有强烈的“时机性”与“善后性”，强调宽缓中有原则，宽恕中有分寸，是真正意义上的解困与松解之道。
overviewEn: Hexagram 40, Deliverance, consists of Thunder above Water. It depicts the moment when danger begins to loosen and tension gives way to release. Water below represents peril, while Thunder above signifies movement and awakening. Together they suggest that difficulties are not permanent; once the right moment arrives, decisive yet measured action can dissolve what once seemed tightly bound. Deliverance is not reckless escape, nor is it passive waiting. It is the art of untying knots after crisis, restoring order after strain, and leading people back to steadiness after fear. The hexagram teaches that when trouble has passed, one should not prolong conflict or cling to a defensive state. If there is nowhere urgent to go, returning and consolidating is auspicious. If there is a task to pursue, early movement brings good fortune. Thus Deliverance is both a hexagram of opportunity and aftermath: it values timing, humane judgment, and practical restoration. True release comes not from force alone, but from acting in harmony with the moment and easing burdens without losing moral clarity.
judgmentZh: 解：利西南，无所往，其来复吉。有攸往，夙吉。
judgmentEn: Deliverance. The southwest furthers. If there is no longer anything where one has to go, return brings good fortune. If there is still something where one has to go, hastening brings good fortune.
imageZh: 雷雨作，解；君子以赦过宥罪。
imageEn: Thunder and rain set in: the image of Deliverance. Thus the superior man pardons mistakes and forgives misdeeds.
interpretationZh: 解卦象征解除困难，雷雨交作，阴霾散去。困难已解，宜宽恕过失，迅速回归正常。
interpretationEn: Xie symbolizes deliverance from difficulty. Thunder and rain clear the air. With hardship resolved, pardon mistakes and return swiftly to normalcy.

lines.textZh: 初六：无咎。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: Without blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 困难刚刚解除，安静无为，没有过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Difficulty has just been resolved. Remain quiet and blameless.
lines.detailZh: 初六曰“无咎”，处解卦之始，说明危局初松之时，不宜躁进。此爻阴柔居下，能够安守本分，不轻举妄动，所以虽在险后余波之中，仍可免于过失。其义在于：问题刚刚缓解时，最重要的是停止制造新的纠缠。
lines.detailEn: The first line says, “No blame.” At the beginning of Deliverance, tension has only just started to ease. This line advises restraint rather than haste. Because it remains modest and does not overreach, it avoids further error. Its lesson is that when trouble has only begun to loosen, one should not create new complications by moving too aggressively.

lines.textZh: 九二：田获三狐，得黄矢，贞吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: One kills three foxes in the field and receives a yellow arrow. Perseverance brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 在田猎中获得三只狐狸，得到黄色箭矢，坚守正道则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Three foxes are caught in the hunt and a yellow arrow is gained. Perseverance brings fortune.
lines.detailZh: 九二曰“田获三狐，得黄矢，贞吉”，象征在解困过程中仍须清除狡黠隐患。三狐比喻潜伏的小人、诡诈与残余问题，黄矢则象征中正有力的手段。此爻居中得正，表明解决问题不能只图表面和缓，还要精准去除核心障碍，方能守正而吉。
lines.detailEn: The second line says, “In the field one catches three foxes and obtains yellow arrows. Perseverance brings good fortune.” The foxes symbolize cunning remnants—hidden problems, deceit, or petty obstruction—that remain even during release. The yellow arrows suggest a balanced and correct means of dealing with them. True deliverance requires not only easing pressure, but also removing subtle causes of disorder.

lines.textZh: 六三：负且乘，致寇至，贞吝。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: If a man carries a burden on his back and nonetheless rides in a carriage, he thereby encourages robbers to draw near. Perseverance leads to humiliation.
lines.interpretationZh: 背着重物却乘坐马车，招致盗贼前来。不当其位，坚持则有困窘。
lines.interpretationEn: Carrying a load while riding in a carriage invites robbers. Holding a position one does not deserve brings humiliation.
lines.detailZh: 六三曰“负且乘，致寇至，贞吝”，是解卦中著名的警示。背负重物而又乘车，德位不称，易招盗寇。其意指人在刚脱困时若急于摆阔、逞能、越分，便会重新招来麻烦。此爻提醒：解困之后尤其要谦抑守分，否则旧险虽去，新患又生。
lines.detailEn: The third line says, “Carrying a burden and yet riding in a carriage brings robbers near. Persistence leads to humiliation.” This is a sharp warning. One displays a status one cannot rightly sustain, and thereby invites trouble. After emerging from difficulty, arrogance, pretension, or acting beyond one’s proper measure can recreate danger. Deliverance must be followed by humility.

lines.textZh: 九四：解而拇，朋至斯孚。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: Deliver yourself from your great toe. Then the companion comes, and him you can trust.
lines.interpretationZh: 解除束缚你的小人，志同道合的朋友就会到来，彼此信任。
lines.interpretationEn: Free yourself from unworthy ties. Then trustworthy companions will come.
lines.detailZh: 九四曰“解而拇，朋至斯孚”，意为先解除细小却牵制全局的束缚，如同先松开脚趾之系，行动方能恢复。此爻阳居阴位，有主动解结之象；当人能够先处理关键的小结点，真正的同道与助力便会到来，信任也随之建立。
lines.detailEn: The fourth line says, “Release your great toe; then friends come, and there is trust.” The image suggests removing the small but crucial restraint that prevents free movement. Once the immediate knot is loosened, support can gather and confidence can be restored. Often a situation changes not by grand gestures, but by resolving one deceptively minor obstruction at the right time.

lines.textZh: 六五：君子维有解，吉；有孚于小人。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: If only the superior man can deliver himself, it brings good fortune. Thus he proves to inferior men that he is in earnest.
lines.interpretationZh: 君子能自我解脱则吉利，以诚信感化小人。
lines.interpretationEn: The superior man delivers himself — good fortune. His sincerity wins over even petty people.
lines.detailZh: 六五曰“君子维有解，吉；有孚于小人”，居尊位而能行解道，说明真正的领导者在局势紧张后，懂得以诚信与宽和安定上下。这里的“有孚于小人”并非迁就，而是以公正可信使原本疑惧、狭隘者也愿意服从。此为以德解纷的上乘之道。
lines.detailEn: The fifth line says, “If the noble one brings about deliverance, it is auspicious; even the petty people trust him.” In the ruling place, this line shows leadership through credible release. The superior person restores order so fairly and sincerely that even the small-minded or distrustful are persuaded. It is not indulgence, but authority made effective through trustworthiness and measured grace.

lines.textZh: 上六：公用射隼于高墉之上，获之，无不利。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: The prince shoots at a hawk on a high wall. He kills it. Everything serves to further.
lines.interpretationZh: 王公在高墙上射中隼鸟，有所斩获，无往不利。
lines.interpretationEn: The prince shoots a hawk upon the high wall and hits it. Everything furthers.
lines.detailZh: 上六曰“公用射隼于高墉之上，获之，无不利”，表示到解卦终局时，仍须果断清除最后的顽固障碍。高墙上的隼象征居高位而伺机的祸患、顽敌或积弊。此前宜宽，此时则须准而狠，一举去患，方能真正完成解。故此爻显示宽严并用，解困之道方得圆满。
lines.detailEn: The top line says, “The prince shoots a hawk on a high wall and captures it. Nothing is unfavorable.” At the end of Deliverance, one final hardened obstacle remains. The hawk on the high wall represents a dangerous element that still occupies a strategic perch. Earlier stages may call for leniency, but here decisive action is necessary. True release is completed only when the last entrenched threat is removed.

judgmentDetailZh: 卦辞曰：“利西南，无所往，其来复吉。有攸往，夙吉。”所谓“西南”，在易理中多主顺、主众、主归，意味着解困之后宜回归群体、回归平实，而非一味冒进。若事情已告一段落，应及时返回本位，整顿残局，此为“来复吉”；若仍有应完成之责，则要迅速处理，故曰“夙吉”。《彖传》云：“解，险以动，动而免乎险，解。解利西南，往得众也；其来复吉，乃得中也；有攸往夙吉，往有功也。天地解而雷雨作，雷雨作而百果草木皆甲坼；解之时大矣哉！”这里点明了解卦的本质：在险难中发动恰当行动，因行动而脱险。其吉不在逞强，而在得众、得中、得时。尤其“解之时大矣哉”，说明解除困局并非简单松手，而是顺应天地生机，使压抑之势转为萌发之机。故判断此卦，应把握“先解结，再谋进”的次序。
judgmentDetailEn: The Judgment says: “Favorable in the southwest. If there is nowhere to go, returning brings good fortune. If there is somewhere to go, early action brings good fortune.” In the symbolism of the Yi, the southwest often suggests yielding, community, and return. After danger is relieved, one should rejoin what is stable and humanly supportive rather than pressing forward blindly. If the matter is essentially over, returning to one’s proper place is auspicious. If something still requires action, then it should be handled promptly. The Tuan Commentary explains: “Deliverance means danger and movement: through movement one escapes danger, and this is Deliverance. Deliverance is favorable in the southwest because going there gains the people. Returning brings good fortune because one thereby attains the center. If there is somewhere to go, early good fortune means that going brings merit. When Heaven and Earth are delivered, thunder and rain arise; when thunder and rain arise, all fruits, grasses, and trees burst forth. Great indeed is the time of Deliverance!” This reveals the essence of the hexagram: not brute force, but timely movement within danger that leads out of danger. Its blessing lies in regaining balance, gathering support, and acting in season. Deliverance is therefore not merely release from pressure; it is the restoration of vitality after obstruction.
imageDetailZh: 《象传》曰：“雷雨作，解；君子以赦过宥罪。”雷雨并作，意味着郁结的天象终于宣泄，空气得以疏通，万物因而舒展。震雷主动，坎水主险，二者交会，不再只是危险，而成为洗涤与更新的力量。君子观此卦象，明白真正的“解”不只是外部环境转松，更是社会关系与人心秩序的修复。因此在处理纷争、失误、旧怨时，不宜一味加重惩罚，而应在原则之下适度宽赦，使积压的怨气得以化开。赦过不是纵容，宥罪不是废法，而是在危局之后先求安定、先使众人复归常轨。此象强调：解除紧张，往往比继续绷紧更能成事。
imageDetailEn: The Image says: “Thunder and rain arise: Deliverance. Thus the noble person pardons faults and forgives transgressions.” Thunder and rain together portray a release of pent-up tension in nature. What was heavy and oppressive finally breaks open; the air clears, and life can breathe again. Thunder gives motion, while water signifies danger and emotional depth. Their meeting transforms peril into cleansing. From this image, the noble person learns that true release is not only the easing of outer conditions, but also the restoration of human relationships. After strain, punishment alone cannot heal what has hardened. Measured pardon, humane judgment, and the easing of accumulated resentment are often what restore order. Forgiveness here does not mean abandoning standards; it means understanding that after crisis, stabilization matters more than severity. The image teaches that sometimes the wisest form of power is to loosen what has become too tight, allowing renewal to take root where fear once dominated.
modernApplicationZh: 事业：解卦用于事业，主先难后易，表示原先的卡点、误会、流程阻滞有望逐步化开。关键不是硬顶，而是尽快沟通、理顺关系、清理积压事务。感情：此卦在感情上多示矛盾缓和、旧结可解，适合坦白交流、解除误会，但切忌翻旧账。若关系已到临界点，主动示弱或释怀，反而能带来转机。财运：财务方面象征风险逐渐释放，债务、拖款、失衡投资有机会进入调整期，宜稳健回收，不宜刚脱困便冒进扩张。健康：解卦对应压力释放、郁结疏通，常提示因紧张、焦虑、睡眠不足所累积的问题开始好转，但仍需通过休息、排湿、舒缓情绪来巩固恢复。整体来看，现代处境中的“解”不是奇迹逆转，而是把复杂问题拆开、把紧张局面松开。最好的应对方式，是先恢复秩序，再图发展；先处理遗留问题，再追求新增长。
modernApplicationEn: Career: In work matters, Deliverance indicates that previous blockages, misunderstandings, or stalled processes can begin to clear. The key is not stubborn force, but timely communication, relationship repair, and the clearing of accumulated tasks. Love: In relationships, this hexagram often points to the easing of tension and the possibility of resolving old knots. Honest conversation helps, but reopening old accusations will only recreate the bind. If the bond is strained, humility and emotional release may create the needed turning point. Wealth: Financially, Deliverance suggests the gradual release of pressure—debts, delayed payments, or poorly balanced investments may begin moving toward adjustment. It favors cautious recovery and consolidation, not aggressive expansion the moment one feels safer. Health: On the physical and emotional level, it often relates to the discharge of stress, stagnation, and anxiety. Problems caused by nervous strain, poor sleep, or prolonged tension may improve, but recovery still requires rest, circulation, emotional easing, and moderation. Overall, in modern life Deliverance rarely means a magical reversal. Rather, it describes the practical untying of complex problems: loosening what is too tight, clearing what is stuck, and restoring order before seeking growth.
historicalStoryZh: 解卦常可联想到周公制礼作乐后的安民之道。殷周之际，天下甫定，人心未安，若一味以威压治世，难以真正解除乱后之结。周公并非只知征伐，更重整秩序、安抚人心、宽和施政，使新政权由紧张走向稳定。这正合“赦过宥罪”之意：大乱之后，首务不是继续扩大战果，而是让百姓从惊惧中缓过来，让旧怨逐渐消解。又如古代良将平乱之后，往往主张止兵息民，而非穷追滥杀，因为真正的胜利并不是把敌意维持下去，而是让局势重新可治理、可生养。解卦所示，正是这种从险中脱身后，及时转向宽简与修复的政治智慧。
historicalStoryEn: Hexagram Deliverance can be illuminated through the political wisdom associated with the Duke of Zhou after the early Zhou settlement. Once upheaval had been subdued, the realm was not truly secure if ruled only by force. Lasting stability required more than military success: it required the restoration of rites, the calming of hearts, and governance marked by measured leniency. This mirrors the Image’s teaching of pardoning faults and forgiving offenses. After great disorder, the first priority is not endless pursuit or intimidation, but allowing people to recover from fear and gradually dissolve old resentments. In Chinese history, wise commanders often understood that once rebellion was quelled, the next task was to stop killing, ease burdens, and let ordinary life resume. Victory was incomplete if hostility remained the ruling principle. Deliverance therefore reflects a deeper kind of success: escaping danger is only the beginning; the higher achievement is turning release into renewal, and power into humane order.
relatedHexagramsNote: 解卦与蹇卦互为综卦。蹇为险阻当前，进退维艰；解则为险难松动，问题开始化开，故常被视为“先蹇后解”的转机之象。与涣卦相比，涣重离散与疏散，解重解除与脱困；与复卦相比，解后的“来复吉”又含回归中道、重建秩序之义。
